# tokens: # `typeof v` returns the type of `v` as a Type literal:
#   - a concrete value -> its exact VType (Integer, ProperString, ...);
#   - ANY type literal -> `Type`. Metatypes are collapsed: the
#     type-of-a-type-literal is uniformly `Type` — there is no
#     ScalarType / NodeType / ObjectType layer. So `typeof typeof v`
# `pathof T` takes a TYPE (a Type literal, or any value whose VType is
# a Type subtype — a Function / Disjunct / Enum value) and returns its
# ancestry path as a List of Type literals, root first, leaf last —
# declared signature `[ Type [:Type] ]`. The last element equals `T`:
# `pathof` does NOT accept a non-type value: `pathof 5` is a
# signature_error. To get the path of a value's type, compose:
#   firstq  — [List] forward-prec — first element or None

note: an Integer literal
negatives too
a Decimal literal
a non-empty string is a ProperString
a single space is still non-empty
the empty string is an EmptyString
a quoted word is an Atom
the unique inhabitant of None reports None
a def-bound reference resolves to the bound value first
a list of mixed nodes is still a List
a map with mixed node values is still a Map
